course_signal: Chapter 1 homework emphasizes operational signal manipulation and definition-based system property classification.

assigned_problem_set.topic: time shift, scaling, reversal, and combined transformations
complex frequency locations for exponentially varying sinusoids

high_priority_concepts: Cross-term logic in energy of x+y and x-y
Time shifting, time scaling, time reversal, and combined transformations
Impulse sifting and impulse product simplification
Impulse integrals and correct impulse location
Differentiation of discontinuous signals with impulse terms
Complex frequency location for exponentially varying sinusoids
high_priority_problem_types: Compute energy of signal sums and differences
Compute power/rms of periodic and scaled signals
Sketch transformed signals from a graph
Express plotted signals using unit steps
Locate complex frequencies in the s-plane
Classify systems as invertible/noninvertible and find inverse mappings
common_traps: Thinking time shift or sign change changes energy
Forgetting power is a time average
Dropping cross terms in energy of x+y or x-y
Doing combined time transformations in the wrong order
Using the wrong support point for delta(1-t), delta(3-t), or delta(t-a)
Forgetting impulse area equals jump size when differentiating discontinuities
Confusing even/odd decomposition with visual halves of a signal
Calling a system linear when the input is squared or multiplied by its derivative
Missing affine offsets such as 3y+2=f in linearity tests
Confusing time invariance with absence of explicit t
Misjudging causality for f(at) when a>1 versus a<1
Assuming f^n(t) is always invertible without checking uniqueness and parity
Calling cosine of the input invertible despite many-to-one behavior
required_example_patterns: Sketch f(t-a), f(t/a), f(-t), f(2t-4), and f(2-t)
Simplify g(t) delta(t-a) by evaluating g(a)
Evaluate integral f(tau) delta(t-tau) d tau
Differentiate a signal with jumps and add impulse terms
Sketch accumulated area integral from -infinity to t
Locate sigma +/- j omega for damped or growing sinusoids
Decompose t u(t) into even and odd components
Test linearity on f^2(t), affine offsets, squared derivatives, and f(t) df/dt
Test time invariance on delay, time scaling, fixed-window integration, and derivative-square systems
Test causality for f(-t) and f(at)
Find inverse relation for y(t)=integral f and y(t)=f(3t-6)

signal_size.label: Signal size: energy, power, and rms
problemTypes: Compute power and rms of periodic signals

signal_transformations.label: Signal transformations and graph literacy
problemTypes: Determine order of operations in f(a t + b)

step_impulse_signal_models.label: Unit step, impulse, derivative, and integral models
traps: Confusing impulse height with impulse area

complex_frequency_signal_models.label: Complex frequencies for exponentially varying sinusoids
concepts: Complex frequency s = sigma + j omega
problemTypes: Locate sigma +/- j omega in the complex plane
traps: Confusing real-axis growth/decay with imaginary-axis oscillation
Forgetting cosine has conjugate frequency pair

even_odd_decomposition.label: Even and odd components
traps: Assuming a causal signal has no even component

system_properties.label: System property classification
problemTypes: Recover input from output when possible
